amendments.body:
BUG: Cancellation during retry backoff reports wrong failure class in run_failed journal event. In engine.rs:4642-4655, when cancellation is detected during the backoff sleep, `fail_run_result(&error, ...)` is called with the original backend error (e.g. TransportFailure). This causes `failure_label()` to return "transport_failure" instead of "cancellation". The pre-existing test `cancellation_between_retry_attempts_does_not_start_next_attempt` (workflow_engine_test.rs:3870) asserts `run_failed.details["failure_class"] == "cancellation"` and will fail.

The sequence: (1) first attempt returns TransportFailure, (2) `will_retry` computed as true (token not cancelled yet), (3) CancelBetweenRetryAttemptsLogWriter triggers cancellation during stage_failed log write, (4) backoff select! detects cancellation, (5) fail_run_result called with original TransportFailure error → writes "transport_failure" not "cancellation".

In the old code (without the select!/cancellation check), the retry loop continued to the next attempt, where the stub adapter detected cancellation and returned InvocationCancelled, correctly producing "cancellation".

Fix: When cancellation is detected during backoff (engine.rs:4642), construct an AppError::InvocationCancelled and pass that to fail_run_result instead of the original `&error`.
amendments.rationale: This is a correctness bug that breaks an existing test. The cancellation-during-backoff optimization changes observable behavior: the run_failed journal event records the wrong failure class. Beyond the test failure, this affects operational observability — operators inspecting failed runs would see "transport_failure" when the actual cause was cancellation.

payload.summary: Reviewed all 5 changed files (retry_policy.rs, engine.rs, error.rs, process_backend.rs, tests). The retry limit increases, BinaryNotFound variant addition, exponential backoff, exit code 127 classification, artifact preservation on timeout, and start_paused test annotations are all correctly implemented. Found one critical bug: the new cancellation-during-backoff path in engine.rs:4642-4655 passes the original backend error (TransportFailure) to fail_run_result instead of a Cancellation error, which breaks the existing test `cancellation_between_retry_attempts_does_not_start_next_attempt` and would misreport failure cause in the journal.
